memoryArtifacts.headline: More About Sir Robert
memoryArtifacts.description:
About Sir Robert Boyd of Noddsdale
Sir Robert distinguished himself at the Battle of Largs in 1263. For his bravery he was awarded lands in Cunningham by Alexander III, King of Scots.
http://home.aust …

memoryArtifacts.description:
Biography

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Boyd IIl於1224年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可時，他的父親Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock19歲，母親Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert26歲。他於1248年之前與Mrs Robert Boyd IIl在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1270年在蘇格蘭艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯享年46歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯吉爾莫爾斯 亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Boyd IIl于1224年出生在英国苏格兰艾斯爾吉爾馬爾諾可时，他的父亲Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock19岁，母亲Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert26岁。他于1248年之前与Mrs Robert Boyd IIl在英国苏格兰艾斯爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1270年在苏格兰艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯享年46岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰易亞斯特 艾斯爾吉爾莫爾斯吉爾莫爾斯 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Boyd
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the island of Bute in the Firth of Clyde, from Bòid (genitive case of Bòd, the Gaelic name of the island of Bute) or Bòideach, denoting a person from Bute. Alternatively, the name may denote descendants of a Gilla filius Boed, who appears in reference to Glasgow Cathedral in the early 12th century, perhaps from the Gaelic personal name Boite, of uncertain origin.
Scottish and Irish: from the Gaelic epithet buidhe ‘yellow(-haired)’. Compare Bowie .
Manx: from Mac Gille Buidhe ‘son of the yellow-haired lad’ (compare 2 above).

variants.name: Bowie
meanings: Scottish: from Gaelic buidhe ‘yellow’, i.e. ‘yellow-haired’, pronounced as two syllables. The equivalent term in Ireland is buí, often Anglicized as boy. The surname may also be a shortened form of Gilboy .
Irish: Anglicized form of Ó Buadhaigh (see Bogue ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Boyd IIl was born in 1224, in Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock, was 19 and his mother, Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert, was 26. He married Mrs Robert Boyd IIl before 1248, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1270, in Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 46, and was buried in Kilmaurs Churchyard, Kilmaurs, East Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Boyd IIl est né en 1224, à Kilmarnock, Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Mrs Robert Boyd IIl avant 1248, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1270, à Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Kilmaurs Churchyard, Kilmaurs, East Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat vor 1248 in Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Mrs Robert Boyd IIl geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1270 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Kilmaurs, Ayrshire, Scotland und wurde in Kilmaurs Churchyard, Kilmaurs, East Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

ko.Kilmaurs Churchyard, Kilmaurs, East Ayrs: 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 에이르실르 킬모르스 킬모르스 c후르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: Robert Boyd IIl(이)가 1224년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르 킬마노c크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1248년 이전 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 Mrs Robert Boyd IIl(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1270년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 킬모르스에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 에이르실르 킬모르스 킬모르스 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

ru.Kilmaurs Churchyard, Kilmaurs, East Ayrs: килмаурс чурчярд, килмаурс, эаст аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Boyd IIl родился в 1224 г. в местности килмарнокк, аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Robert Dictus Boyd  ll 1st Laird of Kilmarnock было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Avellina Fitz Alan Mac Gilbert, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Robert Boyd IIl перед 1248 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1270 г. в местности килмаурс, аиршир, Шотландия в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности килмаурс чурчярд, килмаурс, эаст аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания.
